note: Input is a SPINE slice (a spine + a sprint-01.md), like 01. Each case's fixture is a full spine tree under evals/fixtures/<case>/docs/. At eval time the fixture's docs/ is seeded into the arm's outputs/ (the project root); the skill ADDS docs/design/ and APPENDS docs/spec/amendment-log.json without touching docs/spec/capabilities/. Grade with check_design.py (deterministic, structural + amendment-aware — no LLM judge). Design *beauty* is not graded; the lift is the structured contract the next skills consume PLUS the dual-pass Reconcile. The one deterministic discriminator for a *design* contradiction is the WCAG contrast computation the grader runs over the declared token values.

evals.name: clean-intent
evals.prompt: The Aurora spec spine is already in docs/spec/ and the sprint-1 slice in docs/planning/sprints/sprint-01.md. Run the designer for sprint 1: realize the UX — a tiered design system, per-screen specs, and the DM-ID manifest — all referencing the sprint's REQs, and run the Reconcile pass against docs/spec/design-intent.md. The design-intent is fully stated and its brand color passes WCAG AA, so expect few or no amendments.
evals.expected_output: Under docs/design/: a design-system.md with tiered tokens (a primitive layer + the semantic role vocabulary) referencing the sprint's REQ-IDs; >=1 <screen>.md referencing REQ-001/002/003; an approved/sprint-01/manifest.md carrying DM-IDs; and an owner-tagged DDR section. Because the intent is consistent and AA-passing, Reconcile yields ~zero amendments (the false-positive check). docs/spec/capabilities/ is untouched.
assertions: design-system.md written at docs/design/design-system.md
Tiered tokens: a primitive layer AND a semantic role layer (the governed vocabulary)
design-system references the spine by REQ-ID (traceability, not copied prose)
At least one docs/design/<screen>.md references this sprint's REQs (REQ-001/002/003)
DM-ID manifest present under docs/design/approved/sprint-01/ with DM-IDs
DDR (Design Decision Records) section present in design-system.md
amendment-log.json is valid JSON with an 'amendments' array
Validity: the declared brand #1A56DB passes WCAG AA as text on white (6.18:1)
False-positive check: a clean, AA-passing intent yields no invented amendments (<=1)

evals.name: contrast-conflict
evals.prompt: The Aurora spec spine is already in docs/spec/ and the sprint-1 slice in docs/planning/sprints/sprint-01.md. Run the designer for sprint 1: realize the UX — a tiered design system, per-screen specs, and the DM-ID manifest — all referencing the sprint's REQs, and run the Reconcile pass against docs/spec/design-intent.md. Pay attention to the declared brand color against the project's accessibility floor.
evals.expected_output: The same structured design contract as case 1, PLUS: the dual-pass detector computes that the declared, stated brand color (#7FB3FF) fails WCAG 2.2 AA as button/link text on white (~2.14:1) against the stated 'WCAG 2.2 AA' floor, so Reconcile emits a Tier-2, gated/approved amendment-log.json row citing the brand-vs-a11y contradiction (NOT auto-applied, NOT silently used as text) and proposes a contrast-safe brand for text use.
assertions: design-system.md written + tiered tokens + REQ refs + >=1 screen + DM-ID manifest + DDR section (the structural contract)
amendment-log.json is valid JSON with an 'amendments' array
Validity: the grader recomputes the declared brand #7FB3FF on white = ~2.14:1, below the 4.5:1 AA floor (the planted contradiction is real)
Reconcile caught it: a Tier-2 amendment row cites the brand-vs-a11y contrast contradiction, gated/approved (not auto-applied)
Did not SILENTLY use the failing brand color as text/link — any use is surfaced via the Tier-2 gate

evals.name: derived-intent
evals.prompt: The TeamPulse spec spine is already in docs/spec/ (with discovery notes in docs/discovery/ and the plan in docs/planning/). Run the designer for sprint 1 (docs/planning/sprints/sprint-01.md): realize the UX — a tiered design system, per-screen specs, and the DM-ID manifest — referencing the sprint's REQs, and run the Reconcile pass against docs/spec/design-intent.md, whose brand direction is derived / [NEEDS CLARIFICATION].
evals.expected_output: The structured design contract (tiered tokens referencing the sprint's REQs, >=1 screen, a DM-ID manifest, a DDR section), PLUS the flesh-out component of Reconcile: because design-intent.md's brand adjectives are derived / [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] and it specifies no tokens, the skill proposes a concrete brand direction via a Tier-2 gated amendment-log.json row resolving the marker, and the design-system gives the brand concrete token values it previously lacked.
assertions: design-system.md written + tiered tokens + REQ refs + >=1 screen referencing the sprint's REQs + DM-ID manifest + DDR section
amendment-log.json is valid JSON with an 'amendments' array
Flesh-out: a Tier-2 amendment row resolves the derived/[NEEDS CLARIFICATION] brand intent
Concrete tokens: design-system gives the brand a concrete color the thin design-intent lacked

evals.name: agent
evals.prompt: The Relay spec spine is already in docs/spec/ — it declares Profile: agent-system and carries an agent-contract.md. Run the designer for sprint 1 (docs/planning/sprints/sprint-01.md): realize the UX for the in-scope REQs and generate the DM-ID manifest, referencing the sprint's REQs, and run the Reconcile pass against docs/spec/design-intent.md.
evals.expected_output: Because Profile is agent-system, Phase B designs the AGENT EXPERIENCE, not screens: a tool-surface design that names and traces the agent-contract's tools (inbox.read, kb.search, reply.send, refund.issue, slack.escalate), conversation/turn design, the persona/voice realized from design-intent, error-and-refusal UX, and the HITL touchpoint for refund approval. The DM-NNN manifest is kept but its rows point at tools/turns/refusals. amendment-log.json stays valid (Reconcile targets voice + the agent-contract HITL rows). A baseline designs generic screens/UX with no tool-surface tracing and no DM manifest.
assertions: A docs/design/ agent-experience artifact is written (tool surface, not screens)
DM-ID manifest present with rows pointing at tools/turns/refusals
Tool surface covered: the design references >= half of the agent-contract's tools
Persona/voice realized; error-and-refusal UX designed; HITL touchpoint (refund approval) designed
amendment-log.json is valid JSON with an 'amendments' array
